disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden onset this evening of a sensation of the throat tightening/closing with difficulty swallowing, plus heavy 'crushing' chest pressure and a feeling of not being able to breathe.
trigger_question_examples: What are you feeling right now?
When did this start and what does it feel like?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My throat feels like it's closing and it's hard to swallow, and my chest feels crushed. It came on all at once tonight and I feel like I can't get a full breath.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The symptoms began during a holiday gathering, immediately after a toast to 'family'; the patient volunteers several years of estrangement from their family of origin and finds the holidays very distressing.
trigger_question_examples: Was anything happening just before it started?
What's been going on for you lately?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started right after a toast to 'family'. I've been cut off from my family for a few years now and the holidays really get to me — I was with my close friends, my chosen family, when it hit.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No known food or drug allergies; no new food, medication, insect sting, or other new exposure before the symptoms began.
trigger_question_examples: Did you eat, drink, take, or get stung by anything new just before this?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I don't have any allergies that I know of, and I didn't eat anything new or take any medicine or get stung — it just came over me.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The patient can still speak in full, if breathless, sentences and can take small sips of water, though swallowing feels tight.
trigger_question_examples: Are you able to speak in full sentences?
Can you swallow a sip of water?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I can still talk, but I'm breathless. I managed a sip of water — it felt tight going down but it went down okay.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A pounding, racing heartbeat, shakiness and an overwhelming sense of dread accompany the throat and chest sensations.
trigger_question_examples: How is your heart feeling — racing, pounding?
Are you feeling frightened or on edge?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My heart is hammering and I feel shaky all over, like something terrible is about to happen.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Milder versions of this same throat-and-panic feeling have happened before, mainly around the holidays; never this severe. No prior diagnosis of asthma, heart disease, or swallowing problems.
trigger_question_examples: Has anything like this happened before?
Do you have any history of asthma, heart problems, or trouble swallowing?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I get smaller waves of this around the holidays, but never like tonight. I've never been told I have asthma or a heart problem.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: On gentle, sensitive questioning the patient describes ongoing grief and guilt about the estrangement and low mood around the holidays; when asked directly and kindly, denies current thoughts of self-harm or suicide and denies any plan.
trigger_question_examples: This sounds really painful — how has your mood been through all of this?
Sometimes when people are carrying this much, they have thoughts of not wanting to be here or of hurting themselves. Has anything like that come up for you?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's a lot of grief and guilt, honestly, and the holidays make it worse. But no — I don't want to hurt myself, I don't have thoughts like that. I just want this feeling in my throat to stop.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I'd rather not get into all the family stuff right now, I just need help with my throat.
